pi_items.source: Task description: 'Calculate the Optimal RPM for a wind speed of 12 m/s'
Task description: 'Design a 3-bladed rotor with a 10m radius'
Task description: 'Generate a NACA 4412 airfoil' and verified as real NACA 4-digit designation (4% camber, 40% position, 12% thickness) via Airfoil Tools and aerodynamic literature

Verified via peer-reviewed wind turbine research: NACA 4412 literature shows TSR range 4-7 is typical for small HAWTs; Research shows NACA 4412 turbines with TSR values of 4, 5, 6, 7 in various studies. Value 4.0 is at lower end of typical operating range.

Verified via wind turbine design literature: General HAWT optimal range is 6-8 with 7 being most common. Value 9.0 covers extended operating range and is conservative upper bound for testing. Research shows NACA 4412 optimal at TSR ~7, then decreases TSR 8-10.

privileged_info_summary: Task specifies design of a 3-bladed horizontal axis wind turbine (HAWT) with NACA 4412 airfoil and 10m blade radius. Operating wind speed is 12 m/s. NACA 4412 is a verified real NACA 4-digit airfoil (4% camber at 40% chord, 12% thickness) commonly used in wind turbine research. Optimal Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) for NACA 4412 turbines is documented in peer-reviewed literature to be approximately 6-7, with maximum coefficient of performance around 0.5-0.56. The specified TSR range (4.0-9.0) is reasonable and covers both lower cut-in speeds and extended operating envelope. RPM calculation formula RPM = (TSR × V / R) × (60 / 2π) is mathematically correct for converting TSR to rotational speed. The simulation output is synthetic, generated through QBlade wind turbine design software based on specified parameters.
